goal: Fix Tree/Structure/Lexicon qualname collision: re-key per-callable indexes from bare qualname to (path, qualname) tuple so same-stem files don't clobber each other in view lookups

summary.title: Fix view qualname-collision bug
summary.what: Re-keyed per-callable indexes in Structure and Lexicon from bare qualname (e.g. 'a.f') to (path, qualname) tuple. Bare qualname collided across same-file-stem corpora (a.py + a.js both produce 'a.f') and clobbered each other in _node_by_qualname / _callables_by_qualname / _content_by_qualname / _parts_by_qualname; the later-scanned file overwrote the earlier in lookups, returning wrong AST nodes for the shadowed callables. Updated Structure.cyclomatic, Structure.cognitive, Lexicon.bodies_for_cluster, Lexicon.first_param_clusters, and lexicon.rules.imposters' functions_checked counter to read the new index names. Added a regression test (TestCallableKeyCollisionResistance) that creates a.py + a.js with intentionally different complexity profiles and asserts each callable retains its own metrics. 505 tests pass.
summary.why: Discovered during cross-language cognitive parity testing where the JS callable in a polyglot fixture (a.py + a.js + a.go + ...) returned CogC=1 instead of the expected 4 — because the JS file's AST node had been overwritten in _node_by_qualname by a later-scanned file with the same bare qualname. The algorithm was correct; the index lookup was returning the wrong AST node. Filed as a follow-up at commit time of the cognitive port (commit 2e5192e); fixing now before it bites another view method.
summary.user_goal: Fix Tree/Structure/Lexicon qualname collision: re-key per-callable indexes from bare qualname to (path, qualname) tuple so same-stem files don't clobber each other in view lookups

decisions.chose: Re-key indexes to (str(path), qualname) tuples; rename suffix '_by_qualname' → '_by_key'.
decisions.rationale: Composite key is the simplest correct fix. Renaming the attribute clarifies that lookups now require a path-scoped key, not a bare qualname. Suffix '_by_key' beats '_by_path_qualname' for brevity at no clarity cost.

decisions.chose: Use absolute path (str(c.path)) in the key, not relative.
decisions.rationale: Callables carry absolute paths in c.path. Using the absolute form means lookups don't depend on any root context. Relative paths would couple lookups to whichever root was passed when the view was built.

decisions.chose: Fix as its own intent before any more rule ports.
decisions.rationale: Surfaced as a follow-up to cognitive (commit 2e5192e); fixing now keeps the npath port (next) on a clean foundation. Multi-week kernel-retirement work compounds this bug if not fixed early — every view method that looks up by qualname inherits the bug.

decisions.chose: Add a regression test that exercises BOTH cyclomatic and cognitive on same-stem files.
decisions.rationale: The bug surfaces only when the indexes get clobbered. A direct test of the lookup behavior catches the regression with intentionally-divergent CCX/CogC values across files (Python nested=5/10, JS shallow=4/4).

review_notes: 505 tests pass (was 504). Net change: +1 regression test + 4 file edits.
Rename '_by_qualname' → '_by_key' on attributes is also a behavioral signal — anyone reading view.py now sees that lookups require a composite key, not a bare qualname.
Re-verified the multi-language cognitive parity sweep that originally surfaced the bug: zero mismatches across 11 languages now reproducible with the fix in place.

Lexicon.bodies_for_cluster's existing path-suffix disambiguation (added before this fix) is now redundant in the index lookup but still useful as the outer match (it iterates by (file, simple_name)); kept as is.

architectural_claims: Per-callable view indexes (Structure._*_by_key, Lexicon._*_by_key) are keyed by (path, qualname) tuples. Qualname alone is NOT a unique identifier across files.
Path scoping happens at the index level, not at the qualname level. Records keep clean per-file qualnames; views own the composite-key arithmetic.
behavioral_changes: Structure.cyclomatic and Structure.cognitive return correct metrics for same-file-stem corpora (previously returned the metrics of whichever same-stem file was scanned last).
Lexicon.first_param_clusters and Lexicon.bodies_for_cluster get correct AST nodes for clustered callables across same-stem files.
migration_notes: No external API changes. Internal attribute renames cascade through view methods only.
